description.content: Create and manage field polygon definitions
Returns a list of all registered polygon field boundaries for the account.
Creates a new field polygon boundary for crop monitoring.
Returns details of a specific field polygon.
Deletes a field polygon from the account.
Access satellite imagery and vegetation index data
Searches for available satellite imagery for a polygon within a date range. Returns image metadata including cloud coverage and vegetation indices from Landsat 8 and Sentinel-2 satellites.

query.description: (Required) Polygon identifier to search imagery for.
(Required) Start date for imagery search (Unix timestamp).
(Required) End date for imagery search (Unix timestamp).

response.name: List of available satellite images for the polygon and date range.

description.content: Returns historical NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index) data for a polygon over a specified date range. NDVI values range from -1 to 1, where values near 1 indicate dense green vegetation. Data is derived from Landsat 8 and Sentinel-2 satellite imagery.

query.description: (Required) Start date for NDVI history (Unix timestamp).
(Required) End date for NDVI history (Unix timestamp).

response.name: Historical NDVI data for the polygon.

description.content: Current, forecast, and historical weather data
Returns current weather conditions for a specific geographic location or polygon, including temperature, humidity, wind speed, cloudiness, and precipitation data.

query.description: Polygon identifier (use instead of lat/lon for field-specific weather).

description.content: Returns a 5-day weather forecast for a location or polygon.
Returns current soil temperature and moisture readings for a polygon. Provides soil conditions at multiple depths for precision agriculture irrigation and fertility management.
Returns the current UV (ultraviolet radiation) index for a location or polygon.

Agromonitoring provides satellite imagery, vegetation indices (NDVI, EVI, EVI2, DSWI, NDWI, NRI), weather data, soil analysis, and crop monitoring APIs for precision agriculture. Based on processing satellite data from Landsat 8 and Sentinel-2, the Agro API enables developers to build agricultural monitoring applications with real-time and historical crop, weather, and soil data. Authentication is via API key appended to all requests.
